title: Weather Observation (METAR/SPECI)
description: STORAGE SCHEMA for hourly weather observations. Raw sensor data only. The SDK Observation model is a SUPERSET: it adds relative_humidity (computed from temp+dewpoint) and feels_like_f (NWS algorithm) at load time so existing SDK users see the same 29 fields they always had, plus new additive fields (temp_c, dewpoint_c, qc_field). ASOS measures in Fahrenheit; T-group recovers whole degrees F losslessly.

station_code.description: 3-4 letter NWS station code (ICAO without leading K for CONUS).

observed_at.description: UTC timestamp of observation in RFC3339 format.

observation_type.description: METAR = routine hourly. SPECI = special (significant weather change).

source.description: Data source. AWC = live (2-6 min). IEM = near-real-time gap-fill. GHCNh = historical backfill.

temp_c.description: Temperature in Celsius. For U.S. ASOS sources, Tgroup encodes the integer-°F native sensor reading as tenths-°C (e.g. T0267 = 26.7°C = 80°F); the 0.1°C precision is a coded display artifact, not independent source resolution. For non-ASOS (international whole-°C body group), reflects the source resolution directly. NEVER round at ingest — store source precision as encoded.

dewpoint_c.description: Dewpoint in Celsius. Same Tgroup-as-coded-display-precision rules as temp_c. Modern ASOS sensors derive dewpoint from RH measurement; Tgroup carries both temp and dewpoint as tenths-°C of the integer-°F internal value.

temp_f.description: Temperature in Fahrenheit. For U.S. ASOS Tgroup-bearing rows, this is the integer-valued recovery of the native sensor reading (e.g. 80.0, NOT 80.06). For non-Tgroup rows (international whole-°C body group), this is a float derived from temp_c via celsius_to_fahrenheit. Float-typed for dtype stability across rows; downstream consumers can cast to int when they know the source is ASOS.

dewpoint_f.description: Dewpoint in Fahrenheit. Same integer-recovery-for-ASOS / float-for-international rules as temp_f.

wind_dir_degrees.description: Wind direction in degrees (0-360). RAW sensor, 2-min average, rounded to nearest 10°. Null = calm or variable (AWC 'VRB').

wind_speed_kt.description: Sustained wind speed in knots. RAW sensor, 2-min average of 5-sec samples.

wind_gust_kt.description: Wind gust in knots. RAW sensor, peak 3-sec speed in past 10 minutes. Only present when gusts reported.

altimeter_inhg.description: Altimeter setting in inches of mercury. DERIVED from station pressure + elevation (ICAO Standard Atmosphere, no temperature dependence). METAR body precision: 0.01 inHg. AWC provides in hPa, convert without rounding.

sea_level_pressure_mb.description: Sea level pressure in millibars (hPa). DERIVED from station pressure + elevation + 12hr mean temperature. Less reliable than altimeter (temperature-dependent). METAR remarks precision: 0.1 hPa. MUST NEVER BE ZERO (the original data quality bug).

visibility_miles.description: Visibility in statute miles. HEAVILY DERIVED from forward-scatter sensor extinction coefficient. Quantized to ~18 discrete values (M1/4, 1/4, 1/2, 3/4, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75, 2, 2.5, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10). 10+ reported as 10. Not a continuous measurement.

weather_codes.description: Present weather phenomena codes. DERIVED from IR particle detector classification algorithm. E.g., '-RA' (light rain), 'TSRA' (thunderstorm with rain).

precip_1hr_inches.description: Precipitation in past hour in inches. RAW sensor (heated tipping bucket, 0.01in per tip). Correction applied: C = A(1 + 0.60A). Trace ('T' in METAR) stored as 0.0. Null = not reported.

peak_wind_gust_kt.description: Peak wind gust in knots. From METAR remarks (PK WND dddss/hhmm). RAW sensor peak.

peak_wind_dir.description: Peak wind direction in degrees. From PK WND remark.

peak_wind_time.description: Peak wind time (HHMM format). From PK WND remark.

snow_depth_inches.description: Snow/ice depth on ground in inches. Only available from IEM, not AWC.

qc_field.description: Quality control bitmask from AWC. Bit 0=COR, 1=AUTO, 2=AO2, 3=maintenance($), 4=NOSIG, 5=TSNO. Use for quality filtering.

raw_metar.description: Raw METAR text string. The authoritative source. Used for T-group parsing, PK WND extraction, and debugging.

sky_cover.description: Cloud cover code. HEAVILY DERIVED from ceilometer (single-point measurement extrapolated via 30-min weighted algorithm).

sky_base.description: Cloud base height in feet AGL from ceilometer. Max detection ~12,000 ft.

$comment: ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS: (1) Store BOTH temp_c AND temp_f. Celsius is what METAR reports. Fahrenheit is what ASOS measures internally and what CLI settlement uses. No information loss either way. (2) Removed: relative_humidity, feels_like_f, max_temp_6hr/24hr_f, min_temp_6hr/24hr_f, precip_3hr/6hr/24hr, pressure_tend_hpa. These belong in SEPARATE entities (synoptic_extremes for 6hr/24hr temps, computed_features for RH/feels_like) or are SDK-computed features. (3) PRECISION RULE: Never round at ingest. Store float64 as-is. No explicit rounding in conversion functions. (4) temp_c is stored alongside temp_f because it IS the METAR-reported value and some quants may prefer to work in Celsius. (5) BUSINESS RULES: dewpoint_c <= temp_c, wind_gust_kt > wind_speed_kt when present, sea_level_pressure_mb never zero, sky_base null when CLR/SKC. (6) Source priority: AWC(3) > IEM(2) > GHCNh(1). Training data must match live inference conditions. No COALESCE. One row per observation, rawest source wins. First-reported wins for same-priority (no corrections). (7) Column parity: live SDK and historical API return exactly this 30-field schema. Field names match MVP. (8) RAW-AS-REPORTED: API returns exactly what is stored. All computed features (RH, feels_like, preprocessing) happen client-side in SDK. No server-side transforms on read. (9) PHASE 18 PRECISION FIX: U.S. ASOS sensors observe in integer °F; the Tgroup in METAR remarks is a downstream encoding of that whole-°F internal value as tenths-°C. The SDK recovers the integer °F exactly when Tgroup is present (e.g. T0267 -> temp_f=80.0, temp_c=26.7). For non-ASOS sources (international whole-°C native, GHCNh hourly), the °F is a derived value via celsius_to_fahrenheit and remains float-typed. Cross-source rule: AWC and IEM produce identical temp_c for the same raw METAR (both re-parse Tgroup). GHCNh temp_f conversion is deferred from Phase 18 pending NCEI native-units documentation.
